title: Creamy Beef & Mushroom Pasta Bake
description: A rich, creamy, one-pan beef and mushroom pasta bake that is ready in under 45 minutes.
steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (or 180°C with a fan setting). Slice the mushrooms and chop the onion.
Heat an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Add the minced meat, mushrooms, and onion.
Cook for 7–8 minutes until the meat is browned and the liquid from the mushrooms has completely evaporated.
Stir in the minced garlic, half of the salt, and all of the black pepper. Cook for 1 minute.
Sprinkle the flour over the meat mixture, stir well, and let it cook for about 1 minute.
Slowly pour in the broth and milk, stirring constantly so the flour dissolves into a smooth sauce.
Add the uncooked pasta and push it down so it is submerged. Taste the liquid and add the remaining salt if needed.
Cover the skillet tightly with foil or an oven-safe lid and bake for 20 minutes.
Remove from the oven, take off the cover, give it a quick stir, and top with a handful of grated cheese.
Bake uncovered for 5 more minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbling. Serve hot.
